added_paragraphs: If voice features are available in your market and where you’ve chosen to use a voice feature, we collect and process voice data. Voice data means audio recordings of your voice and transcripts of those recordings.
You can also watch our video about Personal Data at Spotify.
• so we can build AI features such as AI DJ and AI playlist
• to help us enforce our User Guidelines, Platform Rules and Spotify for Creators Community Guidelines.
llm_summary: Spotify's updated privacy policy now specifies that if voice features are available in a user's market, the company will collect and process voice data, including audio recordings and transcripts. The policy also clarifies that data can be collected when users update their accounts, and it emphasizes the use of personal data for developing AI features and improving personalized recommendations. Additionally, the statistics chart now reflects data from 2024 instead of 2023, and a paragraph about data received from third parties has been removed.

modified_paragraphs.after: To evaluate and develop new features, technologies, and improvements to the Spotify Service. This includes development and training of algorithmic and machine learning models, which help us to keep our service safe and enhance our users’ experience. For example:
